_schema_note: Medicare Parts A, B, and D premium rates, IRMAA surcharges, deductibles, and coinsurance. Source: CMS official announcements.
_tax_year_note: IRMAA determination uses MAGI from tax returns filed 2 years prior. 2026 premiums based on 2024 tax returns.

part_b._description: Medicare Part B covers physicians' services, outpatient hospital services, certain home health services, durable medical equipment, and certain other medical and health services not covered by Part A.
part_b._coinsurance_note: After meeting deductible, Medicare typically pays 80% of the Medicare-approved amount; beneficiary pays 20%.
part_b._immunosuppressive_note: For individuals whose full Medicare coverage ended 36 months after kidney transplant (available since 2023).

irmaa._description: Income-Related Monthly Adjustment Amount. Affects approximately 8% of Part B beneficiaries with higher income.
irmaa._mfs_note: Married filing separately has only 3 tiers with accelerated IRMAA surcharges. This applies to beneficiaries who are married, lived with their spouse at any time during the taxable year, but file a separate return.

late_enrollment_penalty._description: Monthly premium may increase by 10% for each full 12-month period that person could have had Part B but did not sign up. Penalty lasts for as long as enrolled in Part B.
exemptions: Covered by employer group health plan while working
Covered by spouse's employer group health plan while spouse is working

part_d._description: Medicare Part D provides coverage for prescription drugs through private insurance plans approved by Medicare. IRMAA surcharge is paid directly to Medicare, not to the plan.
part_d._premium_note: Part D premiums vary by plan. This is the nationwide average for 2026 standalone Part D plans.
part_d._oop_note: Beginning 2025, Part D has an annual out-of-pocket spending cap. Once reached, beneficiary pays $0 for covered medications for remainder of year.

irmaa._description: Part D IRMAA is paid in addition to plan premium. Uses same income brackets as Part B.

part_a._description: Medicare Part A covers inpatient hospital, skilled nursing facility, hospice, inpatient rehabilitation, and some home health care services. Approximately 99% of beneficiaries do not pay a Part A premium (40+ quarters of Medicare-covered employment).

premiums._full_premium_note: For uninsured aged individuals with fewer than 30 quarters of coverage, and certain individuals with disabilities who have exhausted other entitlements.
premiums._reduced_premium_note: For individuals with 30-39 quarters of coverage, or married to someone with 30-39 quarters.

inpatient_hospital._deductible_note: Covers beneficiary's share of costs for the first 60 days of Medicare-covered inpatient hospital care in a benefit period.
inpatient_hospital._lifetime_reserve_note: Up to 60 lifetime reserve days available for extended hospital stays.

skilled_nursing_facility._snf_note: Days 1-20: $0 after hospital deductible. Days 21-100: daily coinsurance. Day 101+: beneficiary pays all costs.

irmaa_appeals._description: Beneficiaries may appeal IRMAA determination if circumstances have changed.
irmaa_appeals._appeal_note: 60 days from receiving SSA notice to file appeal.

life_changing_events.description: You or your spouse stopped working or reduced work hours
You or your spouse had a significant reduction in income
Loss of income-producing property due to disaster, fraud, or other circumstances beyond control
Receipt of settlement from employer or former employer due to bankruptcy, reorganization, or closure

magi_calculation._description: Modified Adjusted Gross Income for IRMAA is calculated from IRS Form 1040.
magi_calculation.formula: Line 11 (Adjusted Gross Income) + Line 2a (Tax-exempt interest income)
magi_calculation._planning_note: Income planning strategies (Roth conversions, timing of capital gains, etc.) may help reduce IRMAA liability.

premium_history._description: Historical Part B standard monthly premiums and annual deductibles.

years._note: Largest year-over-year dollar increase in program history, partly due to Aduhelm uncertainty.
Premium decreased from 2022 (first decrease in over a decade).
9.7% increase; would have been $11/month higher without skin substitute spending reductions.

hold_harmless_provision._description: The Social Security Act provides that Part B premiums for current enrollees cannot increase by more than the dollar amount of the Social Security COLA.
hold_harmless_provision.applies_to: Beneficiaries who have Part B premiums deducted from Social Security benefits
hold_harmless_provision.effect: Premium increase limited to COLA increase amount
exceptions: Beneficiaries enrolled in Medicare but not receiving Social Security benefits

initial_enrollment_period.description: 3 months before turning 65, the month of 65th birthday, and 3 months after

special_enrollment_period.description: After losing employer coverage or when employer group health plan ends
special_enrollment_period._note: No late enrollment penalty if enrolling during SEP

sources.name: CMS 2026 Medicare Parts A & B Premiums and Deductibles
Federal Register - Medicare Part B Monthly Actuarial Rates
